title: Multiple family members with headache and confusion
presentation: Three members of the same family present simultaneously — a 45-year-old father, 42-year-old mother, and 16-year-old daughter — all with severe headache, nausea, and confusion after waking up. They had their gas heater running all night with windows closed. The family dog was also found lethargic. Vitals of father: BP 138/88, HR 106, RR 18, Temp 37.0C, SpO2 99% on pulse oximetry.

immediate: carboxyhemoglobin level via co-oximetry (NOT pulse ox)
urgent: hyperbaric oxygen if COHb >25% or neurological symptoms
gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Pulse oximetry is FALSELY NORMAL in CO poisoning — only co-oximetry gives true saturation. Multiple family members + enclosed space + heater = CO until proven otherwise. 100% O2 immediately — halves CO half-life. Hyperbaric O2 for severe cases.
